summary: Acute increases in heart rate from isoproterenol do not alter established aortic stiffness due to lack of effect on underlying vascular structure or mechanics; instead, they primarily cause short-term blood pressure variations without changing arterial elasticity. The mechanism linking such cardiac stimulation directly to increased aortic rigidity remains unsupported clinically.
